page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It runs on the x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. This configuration comprises 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware acceleration flags such as Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling. The instance achieves average tier results in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core benchmarks fall into the lower quartile, as seen in the Geekbench multi-core score of 3828.0 and stress-ng multi-core score of 3320.67. Memory bandwidth tests also register in the bottom quartile, with uncached read and write speeds of 29.09 GB/sec and 21.83 GB/sec respectively.

The instance lacks local NVMe storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses, meaning storage and external networking must be provisioned separately. While its high memory density supports memory-intensive tasks, its lower multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present a tradeoff for highly parallelized compute jobs. It is suited for workloads requiring moderate compute but higher memory footprints, such as small-scale database operations, caching layers, and lightweight text or language processing tasks using smaller models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8 GB of memory per core. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, it features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 105 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and text processing score in the average tier, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower quartile. The instance does not include local storage or public IPv4 addresses. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate parallel processing requirements, such as small databases, caching systems, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, offering high memory density for moderate workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory-to-core density and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM providing 8 GB of memory per core
Dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration flags
Average single-core performance paired with lower multi-core scaling
No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Suited for small-scale databases, caching, and text processing
